insight:
Yesterday was a solid training day with your 71-minute virtual ride hitting 27.7km at a comfortable 129 bpm average heart rate. Your body responded well, burning 2,951 calories total, and your excellent 45 bpm resting heart rate shows your cardiovascular fitness is dialed in. However, that 6-hour sleep was short of optimal for where you are in training.

Your HRV at 119ms is notably elevated above your 90-111ms baseline, which typically indicates good recovery but could also signal your body is working harder to adapt. Combined with low stress (29/100), this suggests you're handling training well. You can proceed with today's planned session, but listen to your body during the warm-up.

With only 17 days to Chester, prioritize getting 7-8 hours of sleep tonight. Your protein needs are crucial right now - aim for 30-35g at breakfast to kickstart recovery and support your training adaptations as you fine-tune for race day.
